- MS-VECTORS:STORE_SERVICES_FAILURE
- MS-VECTORS:STORE_UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION

## \[Transform\] Parse document

`<ms-vectors:transform-parse-document>`

Parses a document from a raw binary or Base64-encoded content.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document binary</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Raw binary or Base64-encoded content of the document to parse.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document parser</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#MultiformatDocumentParserParameters">Multiformat Document Parser Parameters</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextDocumentParserParameters">Text Document Parser Parameters</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Document parser to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ParserResponseAttributes">Parser Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Transform Config](#TransformConfig)

### Throws

- MS-VECTORS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- MS-VECTORS:TRANSFORM_DOCUMENT_PARSING_FAILURE
- MS-VECTORS:TRANSFORM_OPERATIONS_FAILURE

## \[Transform\] Chunk text

`<ms-vectors:transform-chunk-text>`

Chunks the provided text into multiple segments based on the segmentation parameters. This operation
splits the input text into smaller segments according to the maximum segment size and overlap size
specified in the segmentation parameters. The result is returned as a JSON document containing the
chunked text segments and associated metadata.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Input text to chunk.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Segment Size (Characters)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum size of a segment in characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Overlap Size (Characters)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum overlap between segments in characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ChunkResponseAttributes">Chunk Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Throws

- MS-VECTORS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- MS-VECTORS:TRANSFORM_DOCUMENT_PARSING_FAILURE
- MS-VECTORS:TRANSFORM_OPERATIONS_FAILURE

## Types

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Embedding Response Attributes

Configures the response attributes for the embedding operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Embedding Model Dimension</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Embedding model dimension.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Embedding Model Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Embedding model name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Other Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Other attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Usage</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TokenUsage">Token Usage</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Token usage.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Token Usage

Configures token usage metadata returned as attributes.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Input Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of tokens used to process the input.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of tokens used to generate the output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Total Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Total number of tokens used for input and output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max Buffer Size</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Max Buffer Size</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Error Mapping

Configures error mapping.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ANY</p></li><li><p>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TRANSFORMATION</p></li><li><p>EXPRESSION</p></li><li><p>SECURITY</p></li><li><p>CLIENT_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>SERVER_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>ROUTING</p></li><li><p>CONNECTIVITY</p></li><li><p>RETRY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TIMEOUT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Source of the error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Target of the error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Store Response Attributes

Configures the response attributes for the store operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Metadata Condition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Metadata condition.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Other Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Other attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

Configures the repeatable in memory iterable type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of instances to initially keep in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong> attribute, with an upper limit of <strong>Max Buffer Size</strong>. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower means that the buffer must not expand, meaning that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

Configures the repeatable file store iterable type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Objects</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parser Response Attributes

Configures the response attributes for the parser.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Document Parser Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Document parser name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Chunk Response Attributes

Configures the response attributes for the chunk.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Overlap Size In Chars</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum overlap size in characters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Segment Size In Chars</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum segment size in characters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Multiformat Document Parser Parameters

Configures the parameters for the multiformat document parser.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Include Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to include metadata.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Text Document Parser Parameters

Configures the parameters for the text document parser.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Document Parser</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DocumentParser</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Document parser.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Document parser name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [MuleSoft Vectors Connector Overview](./)
- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure MuleSoft Vectors Connector 1.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure shared libraries](#configure-shared-libraries).
4.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-source).
5.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
6.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
7.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[MuleSoft Vectors Connector Reference](mulesoft-vectors-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure Anypoint Connector for MuleSoft
Vectors (MuleSoft Vectors Connector):

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add MuleSoft Vectors Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, type `mulesoft vectors` in the search field.
3.  Click **MuleSoft Vectors Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure Shared Libraries

To reduce the overall size of the connector and avoid adding dependencies that are not required, all
the optional libraries are removed from the connector and only the required ones can be added
depending on the specific use case. You can use the Mule Maven plugin to add the required
dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file. For more information about how to configure shared
libraries, see
[Configure Shared Libraries](../../mule-runtime/latest/mmp-concept#configure-shared-libraries).

### Embedding Models

#### Configuration

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
	<sharedLibrary>
		<groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
		<artifactId>langchain4j-core</artifactId>
	</sharedLibrary>
    ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
	<dependency>
		<groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
		<artifactId>langchain4j-core</artifactId>
		<version>1.0.1</version>
	</dependency>
    ...
</dependencies>
...
```

### Vector Stores

#### Connections

- Azure AI Search
- AlloyDB
- Chroma
- Elasticsearch
- Milvus
- Amazon OpenSearch
- PGVector
- Pinecone
- Qdrant
- Weaviate
- MongoDB Atlas
- Ephemeral File

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-azure-ai-search</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-azure-ai-search</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-community-alloydb-pg</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-community-alloydb-pg</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-chroma</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-chroma</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-elasticsearch</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-elasticsearch</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-milvus</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-milvus</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-opensearch</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-opensearch</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-pgvector</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-pgvector</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

> [!NOTE] You canât use Pinecone with Milvus or Qdrant.

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-pinecone</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-pinecone</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-qdrant</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>io.grpc</groupId>
      <artifactId>grpc-netty-shaded</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-qdrant</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>io.grpc</groupId>
      <artifactId>grpc-netty-shaded</artifactId>
      <version>1.65.1</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-weaviate</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-weaviate</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
  <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-mongodb-atlas</artifactId>
  </sharedLibrary>
  ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
  <dependency>
      <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
      <artifactId>langchain4j-mongodb-atlas</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.1-beta6</version>
  </dependency>
  ...
</dependencies>
...
```

```xml
...
<sharedLibraries>
		<sharedLibrary>
			<groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
			<artifactId>langchain4j</artifactId>
		</sharedLibrary>
    ...
</sharedLibraries>
...
<dependencies>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
			<artifactId>langchain4j</artifactId>
			<version>1.0.1</version>
		</dependency>
    ...
</dependencies>
...
```

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with MuleSoft Vectors Connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an **HTTP > Listener** source, follow these steps:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the **HTTP > Listener** source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for MuleSoft Vectors Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, select **MuleSoft Vectors Connector** and then select the operation to add.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas, next to the source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
that the connector requires to access the target vector stores and embedding providers.

You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders (recommended),
or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration properties. For
information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to configure them, see
[Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

To configure the global element for MuleSoft Vectors Connector, follow these steps.

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  On the properties screen for the operation, click the Add (**+**) icon to access the global
    element configuration fields.
3.  Choose from these configs.
    - Embedding Config
    - Store Config
    - Transform Config

4.  Fill out the fields depending on the operation type.
5.  Click **OK**.

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for the connector, configure the other required fields. The
required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

For detailed configuration information for each operation type, see
[MuleSoft Vectors - Additional Configuration Information](mulesoft-vectors-additional-config).

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible on the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  operating system console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [MuleSoft Vectors Connector Reference](mulesoft-vectors-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# MuleSoft Vectors Connector 1.1.0 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Code Builder
or Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Code
  Builder or Studio XML editor, or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by
  adding reference statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Anypoint Code Builder or Studio, the XML is automatically populated
  the code with the connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the
  `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-vectors
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-vectors/current/mule-ms-vectors.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:ms-vectors="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-vectors"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-vectors
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-vectors/current/mule-ms-vectors.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
If you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to
this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule4-vectors-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `vectors`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
